name: implies_apply_closure_bridge
reason: implies_apply is external_body bridging p.implies(q)'s TempPred::new closure-wrapped implication to modus ponens — resolves closure identity gap without proof

name: false_pred_unsatisfiable
reason: false_pred = not(true_pred()) should be unsatisfiable — if this fails to verify, the closure nesting in not/true_pred/lift_state prevents SMT from resolving it, exposing a closure opacity issue

name: false_pred_equality_gates_soundness
code:
proof fn phi_4_false_pred_equality_gates_soundness(p: TempPred<int>, r: TempPred<int>)
    requires
        p.and(r) == false_pred::<int>(),
    ensures
        forall |ex: Execution<int>| !(#[trigger] p.and(r).satisfied_by(ex)),
{
}
reason: if p.and(r) == false_pred() holds intensionally, then for any execution it should be unsatisfiable — but the equality is between structurally distinct closures, so this tests whether intensional equality propagates correctly through satisfied_by

name: any_leads_to_from_false_antecedent
reason: derives that false_pred leads to anything under any spec entailing always(true) — tests whether the intensional equality false_pred.and(true_pred) == false_pred holds and whether ex falso quodlibet propagates through the leads_to machinery
